element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player chooses from 24 playable nations to establish competitive identity
Bird's-eye view perspective enables full field awareness; player controls team in single matches
Master System version includes official groups and championship schedule; no qualifying phase

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-based structure with discrete tension cycles per game; tournament arc provides long-term tension escalation toward finals

difficulty_curve.early_game: Initial matches against lower-tier opponents; team selection allows self-tuning via nation choice
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive opponent strength through tournament group advancement
difficulty_curve.late_game: Finals matches against strongest national teams
notable_spikes: Knockout stage advancement (elimination format intensifies stakes)
Finals approach (facing historically strong nations like Brazil, West Germany, Argentina)

notable_level_patterns.description: Master System version replicates official 1990 World Cup groups and scheduling; matches progress from group stage through knockout rounds to championship
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Group stage matches followed by knockout progression

notable_level_patterns.description: Opponent difficulty tied to playable nation selection; stronger nations face stronger competitors; weaker nations face weaker opposition initially
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Playing as Brazil vs. playing as Algeria creates different difficulty curves

notable_level_patterns.description: All 24 playable nations available equally; multiplayer mode enables direct team-to-team competition without role-based asymmetry
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Single-player tournament vs. multiplayer head-to-head matches
